free_text: Measurement of scattering cross sections of Pb-nat at
an incident neutron energy of 2.94 MeV
N-15(p,n)O-15 reaction as source for monoener-
getic neutrons. Pulsed beam with time width of 0.8 to
2.0 ns. Gas target of 3 cm length, with entrance
window of 5 micron Mo-foil. Distance between center
of gas target and pivot point 17.5cm.
Gas target allows background subtraction by gas-in/
gas-out difference measurements and easy variation
of target thickness by changing gas pressure
Energy spread is primary caused by target thickness
Time-of-flight, 12 m flight path of scattered
D2 - D5  10 inches diameter, 2 inches length,
5 detectors D1 to D5 which are always at the same
positions. The distance is always 12.5 deg to the next
detector.If the cyclotron is at 25.0 deg with respect
to detector D1, then data at 25.0, 37.5, 50.0, 62.5
and 75.0 deg were measured at the same time.
ENDF/B-V scat c-s were used for norm (no more details)
Located at the pivot point. Full cylinder of 2.502cm
diameter, 5.002 cm height, 278.260 g mass, chemical
22.455g mass, hydrogen mass content 14.30% was used for
Iteratively fitting a realistic Monte Carlo simulation
of neutron production, scattering and detection process
to measured TOF spectra. Monte Carlo code STREUER was
Angle-integrated cross sections were obtained by
fitting a Legendre polynomial expansion to experimental
hydrogen content of PE sample, uncertainty of ENDF/B-V
hydrogen scattering cross section and counting
statistics of measurement with PE sample;
- Counting statistics of measurement with Pb sample;
With Monte Carlo simulation, data were corrected for
finite geometry, multiple scattering and flux
attenuation in sample and surrounding air.
The measured TOF spectrum was corrected for
empty-target background and scattering from air and
